steps.voice: The Display card. Three things live here — brightness, an optional CAN-driven dimmer, and Night Mode for after-dark driving.

Brightness slider. Five to one hundred percent, with a live preview as you drag — the canvas dims as you slide so you can pick a value that's right for your eyes, not your ECU's spec sheet. The choice persists in NVS, so it survives every power cycle.

CAN Dimmer Switch. Bind brightness to any signal you've got — the headlight bus is the obvious one. When the signal goes high, the backlight drops to your chosen low-light level automatically; goes low, brightness pops back up. No timers, no light sensors required.
Pick the signal, set a threshold, set the dim percent. That's the whole config — three lines of setup for an automatic night response that follows your headlight switch.

Night Mode is a separate thing — it doesn't just dim, it swaps the entire colour palette to warm reds and dimmer surfaces so nothing on the dash glares at 2 AM. Trigger it three ways: tap the Night toggle right here, two-finger tap the dashboard from anywhere, or bind it to a CAN signal exactly like the dimmer.

Per-widget overrides. Any widget can specify its own night colours — expand its Night Mode block in the inspector and pick whichever fields you want different. Leave a field blank and the widget keeps its day colour. Useful for warning lamps that should stay loud even at night.
All three settings — brightness, CAN dimmer config, Night Mode — persist in NVS. Survives reboots, OTA updates, even the dual-slot rollback if a firmware update goes sideways.
